{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head, Business Development and Growth",
  "description": "Adventure Capital Limited is a venture capital investor dedicated to nurturing entrepreneurial ventures and creating unique, high-return opportunities for both active and passive investors. Focusing on industries such as Oil &amp; Gas, IT &amp; Telecommunications, Media &amp; Entertainment, Agriculture, Consumer Goods, Hotels &amp; Tourism, and Manufacturing. The company supports startups and growing businesses at all stages of development. By partnering with visionary entrepreneurs, Adventure Capital Limited strives to transform industries while delivering superior returns for its investors. The firm is committed to fostering collaboration between innovators and investors to achieve mutual success. The firm notably invested in the Nigerian mobility startup Gokada.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head, Business Development and Growth

Job Location:&nbsp;Maryland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	We are seeking a strategic and results-driven Head of Business Development &amp; Growth to lead our revenue expansion and market penetration efforts.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for identifying new business opportunities, building strategic partnerships, driving customer acquisition, and delivering sustainable growth across key markets.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute a comprehensive business development and growth strategy aligned with organizational objectives
	Identify, evaluate, and pursue new market opportunities, partnerships, and revenue streams
	Lead and manage the business development team to achieve revenue and growth targets
	Build and maintain strong relationships with key clients, partners, and stakeholders
	Drive customer acquisition, retention, and expansion initiatives
	Collaborate with marketing, product, and operations teams to optimize go-to-market strategies
	Conduct market research and competitive analysis to inform strategic decisions
	Monitor performance metrics and prepare regular reports on growth initiatives
	Negotiate high-value deals and close strategic partnerships


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Economics, or a related field (MBA is an advantage)
	8&ndash;12 years of experience in business development, sales, or growth roles, with at least 3&ndash;5 years in a leadership capacity
	Proven track record of driving revenue growth and securing high-value partnerships
	Strong commercial acumen and strategic thinking capabilities
	Excellent negotiation, communication, and stakeholder management skills
	Experience working in fast-paced, target-driven environments
	Strong analytical skills and data-driven decision-making approach


Key Competencies:


	Leadership and team management
	Strategic planning and execution
	Market intelligence and opportunity identification
	Relationship management and influence
	Results orientation and accountability


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CVs to:&nbsp;evelyn.olanrewaju@adventurecapital.com.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subjeet of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-18",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-02",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

  },

      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },

Head, Business Development and Growth Job at Adventure Capital Limited




        
    

        
        Adventure Capital Limited is a venture capital investor dedicated to nurturing entrepreneurial ventures and creating unique, high-return opportunities for both active and passive investors. Focusing on industries such as Oil & Gas, IT & Telecommunications, Media & Entertainment, Agriculture, Consumer Goods, Hotels & Tourism, and Manufacturing. The company supports startups and growing businesses at all stages of development. By partnering with visionary entrepreneurs, Adventure Capital Limited strives to transform industries while delivering superior returns for its investors. The firm is committed to fostering collaboration between innovators and investors to achieve mutual success. The firm notably invested in the Nigerian mobility startup Gokada.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head, Business Development and Growth

Job Location: Maryland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	We are seeking a strategic and results-driven Head of Business Development & Growth to lead our revenue expansion and market penetration efforts.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for identifying new business opportunities, building strategic partnerships, driving customer acquisition, and delivering sustainable growth across key markets.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute a comprehensive business development and growth strategy aligned with organizational objectives
	Identify, evaluate, and pursue new market opportunities, partnerships, and revenue streams
	Lead and manage the business development team to achieve revenue and growth targets
	Build and maintain strong relationships with key clients, partners, and stakeholders
	Drive customer acquisition, retention, and expansion initiatives
	Collaborate with marketing, product, and operations teams to optimize go-to-market strategies
	Conduct market research and competitive analysis to inform strategic decisions
	Monitor performance metrics and prepare regular reports on growth initiatives
	Negotiate high-value deals and close strategic partnerships


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Economics, or a related field (MBA is an advantage)
	8–12 years of experience in business development, sales, or growth roles, with at least 3–5 years in a leadership capacity
	Proven track record of driving revenue growth and securing high-value partnerships
	Strong commercial acumen and strategic thinking capabilities
	Excellent negotiation, communication, and stakeholder management skills
	Experience working in fast-paced, target-driven environments
	Strong analytical skills and data-driven decision-making approach


Key Competencies:


	Leadership and team management
	Strategic planning and execution
	Market intelligence and opportunity identification
	Relationship management and influence
	Results orientation and accountability


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CVs to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subjeet of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HR Analyst",
  "description": "Adventure Capital Group -&nbsp;Our client offers sales and services of automobiles. They are a known name in the automobile assembly&nbsp;plants and distribution in Nigeria where the business caters to Auto Sales and distribution.

They are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Analyst

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Position Overview


	The HR Analyst is responsible for providing data-driven insights that are aimed at supporting evidence-based HR decision-making and improving workforce productivity and organisational performance.
	This role aims at analysing workforce, operational, and performance data and identifying improvement opportunities, mitigating risks, and driving efficiency through performance management frameworks, policies, and analytics tools.


Key Responsibilities
HR &amp; Business Performance Analytics:


	Analyse workforce and operational data to identify trends, gaps, and performance&nbsp;improvement opportunities.
	Develop and implement performance metrics and dashboards to improve organisational and&nbsp;operational efficiency.
	Monitor business and HR performance indicators and generate periodic management reports.
	Review performance and operational data sources to identify risks to business objectives and&nbsp;mission accomplishment.


Problem Diagnosis &amp; Solution Design:


	Analyse business and HR problems to determine root causes using quantitative and qualitative&nbsp;methodologies.
	Document identified issues, define problem statements, and outline structured solution&nbsp;frameworks.
	Recommend evidence-based interventions to improve productivity, workforce utilisation, and&nbsp;service delivery.


Performance Management &amp; Incentive Systems:


	Coordinate the implementation and continuous improvement of performance management&nbsp;and incentive frameworks.
	Support goal setting, KPI design, scorecards, and performance review processes.
	Provide post-implementation monitoring, reporting, and optimisation support.


Key Requirements


	A minimum of a Bachelor&#39;s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Statistics,&nbsp;Economics, or related field.
	3&ndash;5+ years of experience in HR Analytics, Workforce Planning, or HR&nbsp;Operations
	Professional Certification from (CIPM, SHRM, HRCI, or Analytics certifications) are an&nbsp;advantage.
	Proficiency in HRIS and payroll systems.
	Competency using advanced spreadsheets and data visualisation tools (Excel, Google Sheets,&nbsp;Power BI, Tableau).
	Applying statistical and trend analysis effectively.
	Demonstrated ability to implement performance management frameworks and policy&nbsp;documentation.
	Demonstrating analytical and critical thinking, business acumen, and attention to detail.

HR Analyst Job at Adventure Capital Group




        
    

        
        Adventure Capital Group - Our client offers sales and services of automobiles. They are a known name in the automobile assembly plants and distribution in Nigeria where the business caters to Auto Sales and distribution.

They are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Analyst

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Position Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The HR Analyst is responsible for providing data-driven insights that are aimed at supporting evidence-based HR decision-making and improving workforce productivity and organisational performance.
	This role aims at analysing workforce, operational, and performance data and identifying improvement opportunities, mitigating risks, and driving efficiency through performance management frameworks, policies, and analytics tools.


Key Responsibilities
HR & Business Performance Analytics:


	Analyse workforce and operational data to identify trends, gaps, and performance improvement opportunities.
	Develop and implement performance metrics and dashboards to improve organisational and operational efficiency.
	Monitor business and HR performance indicators and generate periodic management reports.
	Review performance and operational data sources to identify risks to business objectives and mission accomplishment.


Problem Diagnosis & Solution Design:


	Analyse business and HR problems to determine root causes using quantitative and qualitative methodologies.
	Document identified issues, define problem statements, and outline structured solution frameworks.
	Recommend evidence-based interventions to improve productivity, workforce utilisation, and service delivery.


Performance Management & Incentive Systems:


	Coordinate the implementation and continuous improvement of performance management and incentive frameworks.
	Support goal setting, KPI design, scorecards, and performance review processes.
	Provide post-implementation monitoring, reporting, and optimisation support.


Key Requirements


	A minimum of a Bachelor's degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Statistics, Economics, or related field.
	3–5+ years of experience in HR Analytics, Workforce Planning, or HR Operations
	Professional Certification from (CIPM, SHRM, HRCI, or Analytics certifications) are an advantage.
	Proficiency in HRIS and payroll systems.
	Competency using advanced spreadsheets and data visualisation tools (Excel, Google Sheets, Power BI, Tableau).
	Applying statistical and trend analysis effectively.
	Demonstrated ability to implement performance management frameworks and policy documentation.
	Demonstrating analytical and critical thinking, business acumen, and attention to detail.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Job Position: Customer Service Officer - Technical

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Key Responsibilities
Customer Engagement &amp; Documentation:


	Record and track customer complaints and service requests with accuracy and professionalism.
	Prepare and share estimates/quotations, ensuring timely communication and approvals.
	Manage repair orders from initiation to closure, ensuring proper documentation and smooth handover to accounts.


Service Agreements &amp; Preventive Maintenance:


	Draft and renew Service Level Agreements (SLAs) in line with customer needs.
	Ensure preventive maintenance schedules are adhered to, reducing downtime and enhancing customer satisfaction.


Reporting &amp; Compliance:


	Maintain Work in Progress and Prospect Reports to support management decision-making.
	Participate actively in safety meetings, training, and emergency response systems.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, safety standards, and customer service protocols.


Key Requirements


	Minimum of a Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering or related disciplines
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in customer service or technical support within cooling systems or related fields.
	Strong technical knowledge of cooling products and ability to interpret mechanical/electrical drawings.
	Excellent communication skills (verbal and written) and stakeholder management ability.
	Proven track record of problem-solving and delivering customer satisfaction.


Skills and Competencies:


	Technical expertise in cooling systems.
	Environmental and safety awareness.
	Strong organizational and documentation skills.
	Customer relationship management.
	Problem-solving and initiative.
	Professionalism and attention to detail.

Customer Service Officer - Technical Job at Adventure Capital Group




        
    

        
        Adventure Capital Group - Our client is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Officer - Technical

Job Location: Lagos

Key Responsibilities
Customer Engagement & Documentation:


	Record and track customer complaints and service requests with accuracy and professionalism.
	Prepare and share estimates/quotations, ensuring timely communication and approvals.
	Manage repair orders from initiation to closure, ensuring proper documentation and smooth handover to accounts.


Service Agreements & Preventive Maintenance:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Draft and renew Service Level Agreements (SLAs) in line with customer needs.
	Ensure preventive maintenance schedules are adhered to, reducing downtime and enhancing customer satisfaction.


Reporting & Compliance:


	Maintain Work in Progress and Prospect Reports to support management decision-making.
	Participate actively in safety meetings, training, and emergency response systems.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, safety standards, and customer service protocols.


Key Requirements


	Minimum of a Bachelor’s Degree in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering or related disciplines
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in customer service or technical support within cooling systems or related fields.
	Strong technical knowledge of cooling products and ability to interpret mechanical/electrical drawings.
	Excellent communication skills (verbal and written) and stakeholder management ability.
	Proven track record of problem-solving and delivering customer satisfaction.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Procurement / Quantity Surveyor",
  "description": "Multixept Associates Limited (Chartered Architects) is a multi-disciplinary practice providing services in architecture, interior design, master planning, design consultancy, urban regeneration, project management and contract administration. The practice maintains a fully staffed office in Lagos and derives its strength from a team of established competencies, diverse skill sets,and experience backgrounds.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement / Quantity Surveyor

Job Location: Wuse 2, Abuja (FCT)

Employment Type: Full-time

Summary


	We are currently?seeking?to engage a Procurement / Quantity Surveyor to support the operations of our companies?(BTech ConstructionGroupLtd, and Akida Hills Ltd).?


The successful candidate will:?


	Be responsible for managing project costs and ensuring financial efficiency from design to completion. The role combines cost estimation, tendering, contract administration, and supplier management to keep projects within budget while maintaining quality.
	Provide procurement strategies to ensure cost efficiency, timely delivery of materials, and adherence to project specifications while maintaining quality.
	Make strategic and wholisticdecisionson behalf of the companies.


Responsibilities


	Cost Estimation, Budgeting, Cost Control &amp; Reporting
	Prepare detailed cost estimates for materials, labor, and equipment.
	Develop and monitor project budgets to ensure financial control.
	Procurement &amp; Tendering
	Manage procurement processes including suppliers election and negotiation.
	Prepare tender documents, bills of quantities (BOQ), and rate analyses.
	Review technical specifications for procurement.
	Evaluate contractor and supplier proposals. Also, oversee contract execution and compliance.
	Approve major procurement decisions.
	Work with the Project Manager to draft, review, and manage contracts with contractors, suppliers, and consultants.
	Ensure compliance with legal and regulatory requirements
	Track project expenditures and provide regular financial reports
	Control project financial risks, identify risks, and propose cost-saving measures without compromising quality.
	Work closely with architects, accountants, engineers, and project managers to align procurement strategies with design and construction goals.
	Support decision-making by providing financial insights.


Requirements


	BSc / MSc Degree in Quantity Surveying, Construction Management, or related field.
	4-6 years experiences in construction or architecture firms.
	A chartered Quantity surveyor(preferred)
	Willingnesstotravel(important)
	Strong knowledge of construction materials and methods.
	Proficiency in cost management software (e.g.MS Project).
	Analytical and negotiation skills for supplier management.
	Understanding local and international procurement regulations.
	Excellent communication and teamwork abilities.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV (PDF format)&nbsp;to:&nbsp;admin@multixeptassociates.com&nbsp;using &ldquo;Procurement / Quantity Surveyor&rdquo; as the subject of the email.",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-18",
  "validThrough": "2026-03-27",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Multixept Associates Ltd."
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Abuja",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",

    "value": "196099"
  },

    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",

Procurement / Quantity Surveyor Job at Multixept Associates Limited




        
    

        
        Multixept Associates Limited (Chartered Architects) is a multi-disciplinary practice providing services in architecture, interior design, master planning, design consultancy, urban regeneration, project management and contract administration. The practice maintains a fully staffed office in Lagos and derives its strength from a team of established competencies, diverse skill sets,and experience backgrounds.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement / Quantity Surveyor

Job Location: Wuse 2, Abuja (FCT)

Employment Type: Full-time

Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are currently?seeking?to engage a Procurement / Quantity Surveyor to support the operations of our companies?(BTech ConstructionGroupLtd, and Akida Hills Ltd).?


The successful candidate will:?


	Be responsible for managing project costs and ensuring financial efficiency from design to completion. The role combines cost estimation, tendering, contract administration, and supplier management to keep projects within budget while maintaining quality.
	Provide procurement strategies to ensure cost efficiency, timely delivery of materials, and adherence to project specifications while maintaining quality.
	Make strategic and wholisticdecisionson behalf of the companies.


Responsibilities


	Cost Estimation, Budgeting, Cost Control & Reporting
	Prepare detailed cost estimates for materials, labor, and equipment.
	Develop and monitor project budgets to ensure financial control.
	Procurement & Tendering
	Manage procurement processes including suppliers election and negotiation.
	Prepare tender documents, bills of quantities (BOQ), and rate analyses.
	Review technical specifications for procurement.
	Evaluate contractor and supplier proposals. Also, oversee contract execution and compliance.
	Approve major procurement decisions.
	Work with the Project Manager to draft, review, and manage contracts with contractors, suppliers, and consultants.
	Ensure compliance with legal and regulatory requirements
	Track project expenditures and provide regular financial reports
	Control project financial risks, identify risks, and propose cost-saving measures without compromising quality.
	Work closely with architects, accountants, engineers, and project managers to align procurement strategies with design and construction goals.
	Support decision-making by providing financial insights.


Requirements


	BSc / MSc Degree in Quantity Surveying, Construction Management, or related field.
	4-6 years experiences in construction or architecture firms.
	A chartered Quantity surveyor(preferred)
	Willingnesstotravel(important)
	Strong knowledge of construction materials and methods.
	Proficiency in cost management software (e.g.MS Project).
	Analytical and negotiation skills for supplier management.
	Understanding local and international procurement regulations.
	Excellent communication and teamwork abilities.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV (PDF format) to: [email protected] using “Procurement / Quantity Surveyor” as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Science Laboratory Technician",
  "description": "Lily hospitals Ltd, established since 1986 remains one of the foremost hospitals in Nigeria and within the South-South region of the country. It is the first private hospital in Nigeria to achieve ISO 9001:2000 quality management system certification from the Standards Organization of Nigeria (SON). Also we areconsistently committed to high standards; continuous improvement, service excellence, organizational citizenship, and outstanding work ethics.

Job Position:&nbsp;Science Laboratory Technician

Job Location:&nbsp;Warri, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Science Laboratory Technician with a strong background in chemistry techniques to support laboratory operations within our college.
	The ideal candidate will ensure the smooth running of practical sessions, maintain laboratory standards, and assist students during experiments.


Key Responsibilities


	Prepare chemical reagents and solutions for laboratory practicals
	Maintain cleanliness, organization, and safety of the laboratory at all times
	Assist students and lecturers during practical sessions
	Set up and dismantle laboratory equipment for experiments
	Ensure proper storage and labeling of chemicals and laboratory materials
	Monitor and manage laboratory inventory, including requesting supplies when needed
	Adhere strictly to laboratory health and safety regulations
	Dispose of chemical waste in line with approved procedures
	Perform routine checks and basic maintenance of laboratory equipment.


Requirements


	Minimum of a Diploma or Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Science Laboratory Technology
	Must be registered and licensed with the Institute of Science Laboratory Technology of Nigeria
	Valid practicing license is required
	Proven experience working in a laboratory setting (academic or industrial)
	Strong knowledge of chemistry techniques and laboratory procedures
	Good organizational and time management skills
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team
	Strong attention to detail and commitment to safety standards.


Preferred Qualifications:


	Prior experience working in an educational institution
	Familiarity with laboratory safety regulations and chemical handling protocols.


Benefits


	Competitive salary
	HMO coverage
	Opportunities for professional development
	Supportive work environment.
